meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling. Learn how to use AI-driven debugging to iterate on SaaS ideas, refine your MVP, and ship revenue-generating software faster than your competitors.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing the 'perfect' idea. Instead, use AI to debug and stress-test existing open-source micro-SaaS repositories. By identifying architectural bottlenecks and fixing them with Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform generic code into a unique, high-value product. Focus on shipping, not ideating. Real revenue comes from execution, not theory.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks in 'research mode,' scrolling through forums and waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never hits. While you wait for the 'perfect' project, the market is moving, and your competitors are already capturing users. The paralysis of choice is not a lack of creativity; it is a lack of friction-free execution.

True founders don't wait for ideas; they manufacture them through iteration. By taking existing, functional codebases and using AI to reverse-engineer, debug, and pivot them into new domains, you bypass the ideation phase entirely. We are moving from 'what should I build' to 'how fast can I break this and rebuild it better.'

steps.title: Identify a Friction Point in Open Source
steps.description: Find a GitHub repo that solves 80% of a problem but lacks a critical feature or has technical debt. This is your foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase for architectural bottlenecks and suggest three high-value features that would make this a premium SaaS product.

steps.title: Automated Debugging and Linting
steps.description: Clean the codebase using AI to ensure the foundation is stable before you start adding your custom business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a deep-dive audit of the current module. Identify potential runtime errors and suggest a refactor to improve performance by 30%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'fix all linting errors and optimize the core functions for memory efficiency'

steps.title: Inject Monetization Logic
steps.description: Shift the project from a hobby script to a SaaS by integrating payment gateways and user authentication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft an implementation plan for integrating Stripe checkout into this existing architecture without breaking the current UI flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'add a stripe integration using the current user session logic'

steps.title: Stress Testing the MVP
steps.description: Use AI to simulate edge cases that would crash your app in production, allowing you to patch vulnerabilities early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the core business logic and identify three edge cases that could cause a database failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'write unit tests for all core functions and identify potential security risks'

steps.title: Rapid Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to a staging environment and use AI to analyze initial performance logs for further optimization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review these error logs and provide a prioritized list of code improvements to reduce latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'analyze the logs in the /logs directory and suggest specific code changes'

pro_tips: Treat the AI as a junior dev; always ask it to explain its reasoning so you can catch hallucinations before they reach production.
Focus on 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature parity; if the AI can fix the payment flow, ship it before perfecting the dashboard UI.
Use Claude Code to document your changes as you go; clean commit messages are your best asset when you eventually hire a team.
Don't refactor for beauty; refactor for stability. If the code works and is secure, move on to the next growth feature.
Keep a 'Prompt Library' of your successful architecture queries; this is your competitive edge for the next project.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a platform like Vercel or Railway, set up a simple landing page, and start cold-emailing potential users to validate the problem you just solved.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to build your legacy. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap to ship a product that generates real revenue. The only variable left is your action. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
